title: BD Alaris Infusion Pump - Occlusion Alarms
description: Guide to troubleshooting occlusion alarms on BD Alaris infusion pumps, focusing on external causes before assuming internal failure.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Move the patient to an alternative infusion line if possible. Do not attempt troubleshooting on an active patient infusion unless it is safe to pause or clamp the line.

steps.title: Check the Tubing Path
steps.instructions: Inspect the entire length of the IV tubing for kinks, bends, or pinches. Ensure the tubing is not trapped under bed rails or medical equipment. A kinked or compressed line is the most common cause of occlusion alarms.

steps.title: Verify Pump Cassette Seating
steps.instructions: Open the pump door and check that the cassette is fully seated and locked. Reseat the cassette if necessary; an unseated cassette can trigger occlusion alarms.

steps.title: Check the Tubing Connections
steps.instructions: Ensure that all connectors, Y-sites, and Luer locks are secure and tight. Inspect for partial disconnections, clogged filters, or air bubbles that may impede flow.

steps.title: Assess the Infusion Set
steps.instructions: Confirm that the correct infusion set is loaded according to pump specifications. Some sets have flow sensors that detect backpressure; using an incorrect set may trigger false occlusion alarms.

steps.title: Examine the Pump and Pressure Sensor Area
steps.instructions: Look for any visible obstructions or residue in the area where tubing contacts the pump mechanism. Clean or replace the tubing if necessary—never disassemble the pump internals yourself.

steps.title: Check Pump Settings and Alarms
steps.instructions: Verify the programmed flow rate and pressure limits are appropriate. High flow rates or sensitivity settings may cause nuisance occlusion alarms.

CCR.Complaint: Pump alarming occlusion despite no visible obstruction.
CCR.Cause: Tubing kinked under bedrail and cassette slightly unseated.
CCR.Resolution: Repositioned tubing, reseated cassette, verified alarm cleared, pump returned to service.

helpfulDetails: Tubing inspected for kinks, bends, or clogs.
Pressure alarm history and flow rate settings reviewed.
Final device status: returned to service or labeled OOS if unresolved.

title: BD Alaris Infusion Pump – Door Won’t Latch / “Close Door” Error
description: Troubleshooting guide for BD Alaris infusion pump door latch issues and persistent Close Door errors using logical external checks before escalation.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: If the pump is actively infusing, transfer the patient to another pump before troubleshooting. Do not force the door closed while connected to a patient. This prevents therapy interruption or cassette damage.

steps.title: Power Cycle the Pump
steps.instructions: Remove from AC power. Turn the pump OFF. Wait 10–15 seconds. Power back ON. A software glitch can occasionally cause a false “Close Door” message even when the latch is engaged.

steps.title: Remove and Reinstall the IV Set
steps.instructions: Open the door fully. Remove the tubing/cassette completely. Inspect for twisted tubing, improper cassette seating, and plastic warping. Reinstall per proper loading procedure. Improperly seated tubing is the most common cause of door latch errors.

steps.title: Inspect the Door Latch Mechanism
steps.instructions: Visually inspect the latch hook, the latch receiver, and spring tension. Look for cracks, bent plastic, broken latch tab, and weak or no spring tension. Do not disassemble the door. If the latch does not “snap” into place firmly, the mechanical latch may be worn.

steps.title: Check for Debris or Obstruction
steps.instructions: Inspect the door channel and latch area. Look for dried medication, tape residue, label fragments, and cracked plastic pieces. Clean gently with approved cleaning solution if contamination is present. Debris can prevent the door sensor from recognizing a closed state.

steps.title: Verify Door Alignment
steps.instructions: With no tubing installed, close the door slowly. Observe alignment between latch and receiver. Ensure the door sits flush with the pump body. If the door appears uneven or requires pressure to align, hinge wear or structural damage may be present.

steps.title: Check for “Close Door” Message Without Mechanical Issue
steps.instructions: If the door latches firmly, the door sits flush, and no visible damage exists but the error persists, this suggests door sensor failure, internal switch misalignment, or main board input issue. These are not field-repairable at the department level.

CCR.Complaint: Nursing reports pump displays ‘Close Door’ and infusion will not start. Door appears shut.
CCR.Cause: IV set improperly seated, preventing latch from fully engaging. No mechanical damage observed.
CCR.Resolution: Reseated tubing properly, confirmed firm latch engagement. Performed operational test. Pump functioning normally.

title: BD Alaris Infusion Pump – Air-in-Line Alarms
description: Troubleshooting repeated or unexpected air-in-line alarms on a BD Alaris infusion pump using a structured, external-first approach.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: If the pump is actively infusing, pause the infusion. Move the patient to a backup pump if therapy cannot be safely interrupted. Do not troubleshoot while medication is actively infusing unless clinically appropriate.

steps.title: Inspect the IV Bag or Fluid Container
steps.instructions: Confirm the bag is not empty or nearly empty. Ensure the spike is fully seated in the bag port. Check for air entering through a loose connection. Why this matters: Low fluid levels or loose spikes are common causes of air introduction.

steps.title: Inspect the Tubing from Bag to Pump
steps.instructions: Look for visible air bubbles in the tubing. Check for loose luer connections. Ensure there are no cracks in the tubing. Verify all clamps are open and fluid is flowing freely. If air is present: Re-prime the tubing per facility protocol. Replace the tubing if repeated bubbles appear.

steps.title: Check the Pump Segment in the Mechanism
steps.instructions: Open the pump door and inspect the cassette/administration set section. Confirm tubing is fully seated in the channel. Ensure the tubing is not twisted or misaligned over the air sensor. Close the door firmly until it latches. Why this matters: Misalignment over the ultrasonic air sensor can falsely trigger alarms.

steps.title: Inspect the Air Sensor Area
steps.instructions: Look for dried fluid, tape residue, or debris in the air detection area. Clean the exterior sensor area with approved disinfectant (avoid excessive moisture). Do not disassemble the pump.

steps.title: Replace the Administration Set
steps.instructions: If alarms continue: Replace the entire IV set with a new one. Fully prime the new tubing before loading. This rules out micro-fractures or manufacturing defects in the tubing.

steps.title: Test the Pump Off-Patient
steps.instructions: Load a properly primed new set. Run fluid into a test receptacle. Observe for recurring air alarms. If the alarm persists under controlled testing, the issue may be internal.

CCR.Complaint: Nurse reports repeated air-in-line alarms during infusion despite full IV bag.
CCR.Cause: Visible air bubbles noted in tubing due to incomplete priming. Tubing replaced and properly primed. No further alarms observed during functional test.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced administration set, re-primed line, verified proper seating in pump channel. Performed off-patient functional test. Pump returned to service.

helpfulDetails: Was outlet tested (if using module system)?

title: BD Alaris Infusion Pump – Battery Won’t Hold Charge / Won’t Power On
description: Troubleshooting guide for BD Alaris infusion pumps that will not power on, shut down when unplugged, or fail to hold a battery charge.

steps.title: Ensure the Patient Is Safe
steps.instructions: If the pump is in active use, move the patient to another functioning pump first. Do not troubleshoot a power issue on a live infusion. Patient safety always comes before device evaluation.

steps.title: Verify AC Power Source
steps.instructions: Confirm the outlet is functional (test with another device or outlet tester). Avoid switched outlets. Ensure the hospital-grade power cord is fully inserted into both the wall outlet and the rear of the pump PC unit. If the pump is not truly receiving AC power, the battery will not charge and may appear defective.

steps.title: Check AC Indicator Lights
steps.instructions: Confirm AC power indicator is illuminated on the pump. If no AC indicator is present, swap the power cord with a known-good cord and move to a known-good outlet. If AC is restored and the unit powers on, the issue was external (cord or outlet).

steps.title: Attempt Power-On with AC Connected
steps.instructions: Connect to verified AC power and attempt to power on the PC unit. If the unit does not power on even with confirmed AC power, suspect internal power supply failure and remove from service.

steps.title: Allow Adequate Charge Time
steps.instructions: If the pump powers on while plugged in but shuts down when unplugged, leave the pump plugged into AC for at least 4–6 hours and confirm battery indicator increases appropriately. Deeply discharged batteries may require extended charge time before stabilizing.

steps.title: Inspect for Physical Clues
steps.instructions: Check for excessive heat near battery area, burn smell, swollen casing, corrosion near battery compartment, and repeated low-battery alarms. Any of these suggest internal battery failure.

steps.title: Confirm Module Seating (if applicable)
steps.instructions: Ensure infusion modules are properly seated to the PC unit. Remove and reseat modules and attempt power cycle again. Improper module seating can occasionally create abnormal power behavior.

steps.title: Perform Battery Test (If Supported by Facility Policy)
steps.instructions: If your department performs battery capacity tests during PM, run a controlled discharge test and compare runtime to manufacturer specification. If runtime is significantly below spec, battery replacement is indicated. Do not open the unit unless authorized for internal battery replacement.

CCR.Complaint: Nursing reports pump shuts off when unplugged and will not run on battery.
CCR.Cause: Unit powered on with AC present but shut down immediately when disconnected. After 6-hour charge, battery runtime was less than 2 minutes. Battery failed capacity check.
CCR.Resolution: Removed from service. Replaced internal battery per department policy. Verified normal battery charge and runtime. Returned to service.

title: BD Alaris Infusion Pump – Keypad / Buttons Not Responding
description: Troubleshooting guide for non-responsive or intermittently responsive keypad buttons on a BD Alaris infusion pump.

steps.title: Ensure the Patient Is Safe
steps.instructions: If the pump is actively infusing and the keypad is non-responsive, move the patient to another pump first. Do not attempt prolonged troubleshooting on an active infusion. Patient safety always comes first.

steps.title: Check Power Source and Stability
steps.instructions: Confirm the pump is connected to a known-good hospital outlet. Verify the AC power indicator is illuminated. If on battery power, check battery status. Low voltage conditions or unstable power can cause the processor to partially lock up, making the keypad appear unresponsive. If needed, plug into AC and allow the pump to stabilize.

steps.title: Perform a Controlled Power Cycle
steps.instructions: Turn the pump off completely. Wait 10–15 seconds. Power it back on. Observe whether the keypad responds during boot-up and whether all buttons register normally after restart. If functionality returns, the issue may have been a temporary software freeze.

steps.title: Check for Keypad Lockout or Software State
steps.instructions: Confirm the pump is not in a clinical lock mode. Verify the pump is not stuck in an alarm state requiring acknowledgement. Check if the channel module is awaiting a specific input that limits keypad interaction. Some states limit button functionality intentionally.

steps.title: Inspect for Physical Obstruction or Damage
steps.instructions: Examine the keypad surface closely for cracked overlay, fluid intrusion, sticky residue, or worn membrane areas. Lightly press each button and check for tactile feedback. If buttons feel flat, mushy, or inconsistent, the membrane may be failing.

steps.title: Reseat Modules (If Applicable)
steps.instructions: If this is part of a modular Alaris system, power down the unit, remove and reseat the affected module, and inspect connection rails for debris or bent contacts. Poor communication between modules can mimic keypad failure.

steps.title: Observe Display Behavior
steps.instructions: Determine whether the screen is responsive while the keypad is not, whether the screen is frozen as well, and whether alarm tones are functioning. If both display and keypad are unresponsive, suspect system-level freeze rather than keypad-only failure.

steps.title: Check for Signs of Fluid Intrusion
steps.instructions: Inspect around the keypad seam. Check underside for corrosion or staining. Look for unusual odor or residue. Fluid damage is a common cause of keypad failure in infusion pumps. Do not open the unit in clinical areas.

CCR.Complaint: Nursing reported keypad buttons not responding on Channel A. Unable to program infusion.
CCR.Cause: Power stable. Unit rebooted. Keypad remained non-responsive. Physical inspection revealed worn and partially lifted keypad membrane. No response to multiple button presses.
CCR.Resolution: Removed pump from service. Labeled Out of Service. Sent to vendor for keypad replacement and internal inspection.

helpfulDetails: Battery status at time of issue

title: BD Alaris Infusion Pump - Channel Module Not Recognized by PC Unit
description: Troubleshooting guide for situations where a BD Alaris Channel Module is connected but not recognized by the PC Unit.

steps.title: Verify the Channel Module is Properly Attached to the PC Unit
steps.instructions: Remove the module and reattach it firmly to the side of the PC Unit. The module should slide fully into place with no visible gap between units.

steps.title: Check for Proper Locking Engagement
steps.instructions: Ensure the mechanical latch between the PC Unit and Channel Module engages correctly. If the latch does not lock, the internal communication contacts may not align.

steps.title: Confirm the PC Unit is Powered On
steps.instructions: A Channel Module will not initialize if the PC Unit is off or still booting. Verify the PC Unit screen is active and fully operational.

steps.title: Observe the Module Power Indicators
steps.instructions: Look for indicator lights on the Channel Module when connected. If no lights appear, the module may not be receiving power from the PC Unit.

steps.title: Inspect Electrical Contacts
steps.instructions: Remove the module and inspect the communication/power contacts on both the PC Unit and module. Look for bent pins, debris or fluid contamination, and corrosion or damage.

steps.title: Clean Contacts if Necessary
steps.instructions: If debris is present, gently clean contacts using approved cleaning methods (such as alcohol wipes) and allow them to fully dry before reconnecting.

steps.title: Swap with a Known Good Channel Module
steps.instructions: Connect another Channel Module to the same PC Unit. If the new module is recognized, the original module may be defective. If neither module is recognized, the PC Unit interface may be faulty.

steps.title: Test the Suspect Module on Another PC Unit
steps.instructions: Attach the suspected module to a different PC Unit. This helps determine whether the issue follows the module or remains with the original PC Unit.

steps.title: Check for Physical Damage
steps.instructions: Inspect the module housing and connection rails for cracks, warping, or signs of impact that could prevent proper alignment.

steps.title: Power Cycle the PC Unit
steps.instructions: Power down the PC Unit completely and restart it. Occasionally the system may fail to initialize a module until the system resets.

CCR.Complaint: Nursing reported that the Alaris Channel Module would not appear on the PC Unit display and infusion could not be started.
CCR.Cause: During inspection, the Channel Module communication contacts were contaminated with dried fluid residue, preventing proper electrical connection.
CCR.Resolution: Contacts were cleaned and the module was reseated. The PC Unit successfully recognized the Channel Module and the device passed operational checks before being returned to service.

helpfulDetails: Module swapped with known good unit
Final device status (returned to service or sent for repair)

title: BD Alaris Infusion Pump – Pump Fails Self-Test During Power-On
description: Troubleshooting a BD Alaris infusion pump that fails the startup self-test, including power, module, battery, and external condition checks.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Verify the infusion pump is not connected to a patient before troubleshooting. If the device failed during use, move the patient to another functioning infusion pump. Ensure therapy continues safely before evaluating the device.

steps.title: Verify AC Power Source
steps.instructions: Confirm the pump is connected to a known working hospital-grade outlet. Test the outlet with another device if needed. Ensure the AC power cord is firmly seated in both the pump and the wall outlet. Check for visible damage to the power cord. Low or unstable input power can interrupt the startup process and cause the self-test to fail.

steps.title: Observe Startup Error Messages or Codes
steps.instructions: Power the pump on and carefully observe any error messages or codes displayed during the self-test. Document any message shown on the display. Note whether the failure occurs immediately or later in the boot sequence. Specific error codes may indicate issues with memory, modules, battery systems, or internal communication.

steps.title: Remove All Attached Modules
steps.instructions: Disconnect any attached Channel Modules or specialty modules from the PC Unit. Remove all modules from the mounting rail. Power the PC Unit on by itself. A faulty module can cause the PC Unit to fail startup diagnostics or communication checks.

steps.title: Perform a Power Cycle
steps.instructions: Completely power down the device. Disconnect AC power. Wait approximately 30 seconds. Reconnect power and attempt to start the device again. Temporary software or memory faults can sometimes clear after a full power reset.

steps.title: Check the Battery Condition
steps.instructions: If the pump contains an internal battery: verify the battery is installed properly. Look for battery warning indicators. Attempt startup while connected to AC power. A failing battery can sometimes interrupt power stabilization during the self-test process.

steps.title: Inspect for External Damage or Contamination
steps.instructions: Perform a quick visual inspection of the unit. Check for liquid intrusion, cracked housing, burnt smell, unusual heat, and debris in connectors. Physical damage or contamination may interfere with internal components or connectors during system checks.

steps.title: Test With a Different AC Power Cord
steps.instructions: Swap the AC power cord with a known working cord if available. A failing or intermittent power cord may supply inconsistent power during the startup sequence.

CCR.Complaint: Nursing reported the BD Alaris pump fails during startup and displays a system error during the self-test.
CCR.Cause: Unit repeatedly failed power-on self-test with modules attached. After module removal and power cycle, the failure persisted, indicating likely internal system fault.
CCR.Resolution: Removed pump from service, labeled Out of Service, and sent to Clinical Engineering shop/vendor for further diagnostic evaluation.

helpfulDetails: Any unusual sounds, smells, or heat
Final device status (removed from service or repaired)

title: BD Alaris Infusion Pump – Upstream Occlusion / Check IV Set Alarms
description: Troubleshooting guide for BD Alaris infusion pumps generating upstream occlusion or Check IV Set alarms during infusion setup.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: If the pump is currently connected to a patient, transfer the infusion to a backup pump or alternate delivery method before troubleshooting. Do not continue troubleshooting while the pump is delivering medication to a patient.

steps.title: Confirm the Alarm Message
steps.instructions: Verify the exact alarm displayed on the pump screen. Common related alarms include Upstream Occlusion, Check IV Set, and Flow Error. Document the exact alarm wording for the work order.

steps.title: Check the IV Fluid Container
steps.instructions: Ensure the IV bag or bottle is not empty or nearly empty. Low fluid levels can introduce air or reduce pressure in the line, which may trigger upstream flow alarms.

steps.title: Inspect Roller Clamps and Slide Clamps
steps.instructions: Follow the tubing from the fluid container to the pump and verify that roller clamps are fully open and slide clamps are not partially closed. Partially closed clamps are a very common cause of upstream occlusion alarms.

steps.title: Check for Kinks or Pinched Tubing
steps.instructions: Inspect the IV tubing between the fluid container and the pump for sharp bends, kinks, tubing caught under equipment, or compression from bed rails or poles. Any restriction in the tubing can cause the pump to detect reduced upstream flow.

steps.title: Verify Proper IV Set Installation
steps.instructions: Open the pump door and verify the IV administration set is installed correctly. Check that the tubing is seated correctly in the pumping channel, the set is aligned with the pump’s guides, and the cassette or tubing segment is not twisted.

steps.title: Check the Drip Chamber
steps.instructions: Ensure the drip chamber is filled to the proper level, usually about half full, and is not collapsed or overfilled. A collapsed drip chamber can restrict flow from the fluid container.

steps.title: Inspect the Spike Connection
steps.instructions: Confirm the IV set spike is fully inserted into the fluid container port. Look for loose spike connections, air entering the line, or damaged ports that could reduce fluid flow.

steps.title: Try a Different IV Set
steps.instructions: Replace the administration set with a new compatible IV set. Defective tubing, manufacturing issues, or internal tubing obstructions can cause persistent alarms.

steps.title: Test with a Known Good Setup
steps.instructions: Connect a new IV set, hang a full fluid bag, prime the line, and run the pump briefly off-patient. If the pump runs normally, the original setup was likely the cause.

CCR.Complaint: Nursing reported repeated 'Upstream Occlusion / Check IV Set' alarms during infusion setup.
CCR.Cause: Inspection revealed the roller clamp on the IV tubing was partially closed, restricting flow to the pump.
CCR.Resolution: Opened the roller clamp fully and verified normal infusion operation. Alarm cleared and pump returned to service.

helpfulDetails: Bag level at time of alarm
Test results with new IV set

title: BD Alaris Infusion Pump – Flow Rate Inaccurate or Infusion Not Delivering
description: Troubleshooting inaccurate infusion flow or situations where a BD Alaris infusion pump appears to run but fluid is not being delivered.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: If the pump is connected to a patient, transfer the infusion to another functioning pump before beginning troubleshooting. Ensure medication delivery is not interrupted during evaluation. Never perform troubleshooting that could affect therapy while the device is connected to a patient.

steps.title: Verify the reported issue
steps.instructions: Confirm what the user observed. Determine whether the pump appeared to run but fluid was not moving, the infusion completed too quickly, or the medication bag level did not change as expected. Understanding the complaint helps determine where to start.

steps.title: Confirm the programmed infusion settings
steps.instructions: Check the programmed rate, volume to be infused (VTBI), and infusion mode. Incorrect programming (such as a low rate or incorrect units) can make the infusion appear inaccurate.

steps.title: Inspect the IV set installation in the pump channel
steps.instructions: Open the pump door and confirm the IV tubing is correctly seated in the pumping mechanism. Tubing that is misaligned, twisted, or not fully seated can cause inconsistent flow or prevent the pump from moving fluid properly.

steps.title: Verify the correct IV set type is being used
steps.instructions: Ensure the tubing set is compatible with the Alaris pump and matches the set type configured in the pump. Using the wrong tubing set may affect flow accuracy.

steps.title: Check for kinks, clamps, or restrictions in the IV line
steps.instructions: Inspect the tubing from the IV bag to the distal end. Kinks, closed roller clamps, or tight bends can restrict flow and cause inaccurate delivery.

steps.title: Inspect the drip chamber and fluid source
steps.instructions: Ensure the IV bag contains fluid and the drip chamber is properly primed. An empty bag, partially primed line, or collapsed bag can cause the pump to appear to run without delivering fluid.

steps.title: Check for upstream or downstream occlusions
steps.instructions: Verify that the line is not occluded between the bag and pump (upstream) or between the pump and patient (downstream). Even partial occlusions may slow flow without immediately triggering an alarm.

steps.title: Confirm the pump door is fully closed and latched
steps.instructions: A partially closed door may affect the pumping mechanism and lead to inconsistent delivery.

steps.title: Run a test infusion using a known-good IV set
steps.instructions: If available, install a new compatible IV set and run a controlled test infusion using a test bag or water source. This helps determine whether the issue was related to the original tubing set.

steps.title: Observe pump operation during the test infusion
steps.instructions: Monitor whether the pump rotor moves smoothly and whether fluid visibly progresses through the tubing. Irregular rotor movement or lack of fluid movement may indicate an internal pump mechanism issue.

CCR.Complaint: Nurse reported infusion running at 50 mL/hr but medication bag level not decreasing and patient not receiving expected volume.
CCR.Cause: IV tubing found partially kinked near pump channel causing restricted flow.
CCR.Resolution: Tubing repositioned and replaced with new compatible IV set. Pump tested and confirmed normal flow.

title: BD Alaris Infusion Pump - Cassette / IV Set Not Recognized
description: Troubleshoot situations where the BD Alaris pump does not recognize the installed cassette or IV set, preventing infusion.

steps.title: Confirm the Pump Is Not Connected to an Active Patient
steps.instructions: Before troubleshooting, ensure the patient has been safely moved to another infusion pump if therapy is required. Troubleshooting should not be performed while the device is delivering medication to a patient.

steps.title: Verify the Correct IV Set Type
steps.instructions: Confirm that the IV set installed is approved for use with the BD Alaris pump and matches the programmed infusion mode. Incorrect or incompatible sets may not be recognized by the pump.

steps.title: Open and Re-seat the IV Set
steps.instructions: Open the pump door and fully remove the cassette or IV set. Reinstall the set carefully according to the loading diagram on the pump. Ensure the cassette is fully seated, tubing correctly routed, and no twists or misalignment are present.

steps.title: Inspect the Cassette Area for Debris or Fluid
steps.instructions: Visually inspect the cassette detection area inside the pump for dried fluid residue, dust, debris, or bent plastic guides. Debris or contamination may prevent the pump sensors from detecting the cassette correctly.

steps.title: Inspect the Pump Door and Latch
steps.instructions: Close the pump door slowly and ensure it fully latches. Check for door not fully closing, latch misalignment, or obstructions preventing closure. The pump must detect a fully closed door before recognizing the IV set.

steps.title: Try a New IV Set
steps.instructions: Remove the current disposable set and install a new IV set to rule out manufacturing defects, worn plastic features, or damage during storage/handling.

steps.title: Restart the Pump
steps.instructions: Power the pump off, wait a few seconds, then power it back on. Restarting allows the system to reinitialize cassette detection sensors.

steps.title: Test the Channel Module on Another PC Unit (If Available)
steps.instructions: Connect the channel module to another Alaris PC Unit to determine if the issue follows the module. This helps identify whether the problem is with the module or the PC unit interface.

CCR.Complaint: Nursing staff reported that the BD Alaris pump would not recognize the installed IV set and displayed a “Check IV Set” message.
CCR.Cause: Inspection found the cassette was not fully seated in the pump channel and the door was not fully latched.
CCR.Resolution: Reinstalled IV set correctly and verified proper door closure. Pump recognized the set and passed functional testing.

helpfulDetails: Channel tested on alternate PC unit
Final device status (returned to service or sent for repair)

title: BD Alaris Infusion Pump – Communication Failure with EMR / Guardrails Library
description: Guide for troubleshooting BD Alaris infusion pumps that fail to communicate with EMR or Guardrails Library.

steps.title: Verify Network Connection
steps.instructions: Check that the pump is properly connected to the hospital network (wired or Wi-Fi). Ensure the network port or access point is functional; try a different network jack or Wi-Fi access point. Why: Communication failures are often caused by simple network connectivity issues.

steps.title: Check Ethernet or Wi-Fi Cables and Modules
steps.instructions: Inspect cables for damage and reseat connections. For Wi-Fi, ensure signal strength is adequate. Why: Loose or damaged cables prevent data transmission.

steps.title: Confirm IP Address and Network Settings
steps.instructions: Navigate to the pump’s network settings. Verify that the IP, subnet mask, and gateway are correct and consistent with hospital network policies. Why: Incorrect network settings block EMR and Guardrails communication.

steps.title: Verify EMR Server Status
steps.instructions: Ensure the EMR interface server is online and reachable. Ping the EMR server from a network workstation if possible. Why: Pump communication depends on a functional EMR interface.

steps.title: Check Guardrails Library Version
steps.instructions: Confirm the pump has the latest Guardrails Library loaded. If outdated, initiate a library update from the configured source. Why: Version mismatches can prevent downloads and block EMR communication.

steps.title: Inspect Firewall or Security Software Settings
steps.instructions: Verify the network path between pump and EMR server is not blocked by firewalls or security policies. Why: Network restrictions can silently block communication.

steps.title: Restart Pump and Network Hardware
steps.instructions: Power cycle the infusion pump. If safe, restart nearby network switches or access points serving the pump. Why: Temporary network glitches or pump software errors can resolve with a reboot.

CCR.Complaint: Pump fails to communicate with EMR; infusion orders and Guardrails Library not accessible.
CCR.Cause: Network settings verified; EMR server reachable; pump fails to download latest library; issue may reside in internal network module.
CCR.Resolution: Pump removed from service, labeled “Out of Service,” sent for bench evaluation or manufacturer repair.

helpfulDetails: Final status of pump (Out of Service)

title: BD Alaris Infusion Pump - Guardrails Drug Library Not Loading or Corrupted
description: Guide to troubleshoot BD Alaris pumps that fail to load or display errors with the Guardrails Drug Library.

steps.title: Verify Pump Power and Boot
steps.instructions: Ensure the pump powers on fully without error messages. A failed self-test can prevent the library from loading correctly. Why: Without full boot, the pump cannot access or display the library.

steps.title: Check Network / EMR Connection
steps.instructions: Confirm the pump is connected to the hospital network (wired or wireless) and has IP access if using network-based library updates. Ping the EMR server or library server to confirm connectivity. Why: Library downloads require network communication; failures here often look like corrupted libraries.

steps.title: Verify Library File on Update Media
steps.instructions: Check the USB drive or SD card used for library updates. Ensure it contains the correct, uncorrupted Guardrails Library file. Reformat media and reload the file if necessary. Why: A corrupt or incomplete file transfer can prevent loading.

steps.title: Check Pump Software Version
steps.instructions: Ensure pump firmware/software is compatible with the current Guardrails Library version. Why: Mismatched software can cause loading failures or errors.

steps.title: Attempt Manual Library Load
steps.instructions: Insert the update media (USB/SD) into the pump. Follow the pump prompts to manually load the library. Observe any error codes displayed during the process. Why: Confirms whether the pump can process the library independently of EMR connectivity.

steps.title: Swap to Known-Good Library
steps.instructions: If available, try a previously verified library file to determine whether the problem is with the pump or the library file. Why: Differentiates between pump corruption and library corruption.

steps.title: Document Any Error Messages
steps.instructions: Record any error codes, messages, or unusual behaviors for CCR documentation and possible vendor escalation.

CCR.Complaint: Pump displays error ‘Drug Library Not Loaded’ and blocks infusion orders.
CCR.Cause: Confirmed that network connection is stable and update media file is correct; library fails to load manually, suggesting internal corruption.
CCR.Resolution: Pump removed from service, labeled Out of Service, sent to BD for repair/bench evaluation.

helpfulDetails: Pump software version and serial number
USB/SD media type and last update date
Error codes displayed during manual load attempt

title: BD Alaris Infusion Pump - Pump Reboots or Freezes During Operation
description: Guide for troubleshooting BD Alaris pumps that unexpectedly reboot or freeze during operation, focusing on power, battery, cables, environment, and software before internal evaluation.

steps.title: Verify Power Source
steps.instructions: Check the wall outlet with a verified device or a calibrated outlet tester. Ensure the pump is properly seated on its power supply or charging station. Why: Unstable power can cause unexpected reboots.

steps.title: Inspect the Battery
steps.instructions: Check battery level and status; replace with a known good battery if low or suspect. Ensure battery contacts are clean and free of corrosion. Why: A failing battery can cause freezing or intermittent operation.

steps.title: Examine External Cables and Accessories
steps.instructions: Remove all attached modules, IV sets, and communication cables. Reconnect modules one at a time and test operation. Why: A faulty channel module or loose connection can disrupt pump operation.

steps.title: Check for Environmental Factors
steps.instructions: Ensure the pump is operating within manufacturer-specified temperature and humidity ranges. Remove the pump from direct sunlight or heat sources. Why: Extreme temperatures can cause the microprocessor to reset.

steps.title: Restart and Monitor
steps.instructions: Power cycle the pump: turn off, wait 30 seconds, and power on. Observe startup self-test messages and log any warnings. Why: Temporary software glitches can sometimes be resolved with a reboot.

steps.title: Software / Guardrails Check
steps.instructions: Confirm the pump software version and Guardrails library are current and not corrupted. If a recent update was performed, verify that it completed successfully. Why: Software or library corruption can lead to mid-operation freezes.

CCR.Complaint: Pump freezes mid-infusion or reboots unexpectedly during operation.
CCR.Cause: External inspection revealed no cable issues, battery fully charged, power stable, but pump continues to reboot—likely internal software or hardware issue.
CCR.Resolution: Device removed from service, labeled Out of Service, sent to vendor/biomed bench for evaluation.

helpfulDetails: Modules or accessories attached during incident
Any unusual heat, sounds, or error codes
Software version and Guardrails library status

title: BD Alaris Infusion Pump - Network Connectivity Failure (Wireless / Ethernet)
description: Guide for troubleshooting network connectivity failures on BD Alaris infusion pumps via wireless or Ethernet.

steps.title: Verify Network Settings on Pump
steps.instructions: Check that the pump’s network configuration matches hospital requirements (SSID, VLAN, IP settings). Incorrect configuration is a common cause of connectivity failure.

steps.title: Inspect Physical Connections (Ethernet Only)
steps.instructions: Ensure the Ethernet cable is fully seated at both the pump and wall port. Swap the cable with a known working one to rule out cable failure.

steps.title: Check Wi-Fi Signal and Coverage
steps.instructions: Confirm the pump is within range of the access point. Observe signal strength indicator; weak signal can cause intermittent connectivity.

steps.title: Verify Network Availability
steps.instructions: Ping the network or test another device at the same port or Wi-Fi access point to ensure connectivity. If other devices fail, the issue may be with the network rather than the pump.

steps.title: Restart Network Services on Pump
steps.instructions: Power cycle the pump to refresh network connection. Some network errors are resolved with a simple reboot.

steps.title: Check for IP Conflicts or DHCP Issues
steps.instructions: Ensure the pump is receiving a valid IP address. If using static IPs, confirm no duplicate IPs exist.

steps.title: Observe Network Indicators and Logs
steps.instructions: Look for any network error codes, flashing LEDs, or system messages. Document any codes for escalation.

CCR.Complaint: Pump not communicating with EMR; wireless connection failing.
CCR.Cause: Pump unable to connect; verified network settings, Wi-Fi coverage, and Ethernet cable; other devices on network functional.
CCR.Resolution: Pump removed from service, labeled Out of Service, sent to Clinical Engineering for network module evaluation.

helpfulDetails: SSID name and signal strength if wireless

title: BD Alaris Infusion Pump - “System Error” or Unrecoverable Fault Codes
description: Guide to troubleshoot BD Alaris infusion pumps displaying system errors or unrecoverable fault codes.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Immediately move the patient to a functional pump if the current device is in use. Do not attempt to administer therapy with a device showing unrecoverable errors.

steps.title: Power Cycle the Pump
steps.instructions: Disconnect the pump from power and remove the battery if applicable. Wait at least 30 seconds before reconnecting power. Observe if the system error persists.

steps.title: Check Power Sources and Connections
steps.instructions: Verify the battery is fully charged and seated correctly. Ensure the AC adapter and cords are intact and properly connected. Inspect for visible damage to connectors or ports that may cause faults.

steps.title: Inspect the Pump for Environmental or External Issues
steps.instructions: Confirm the pump is not exposed to extreme temperatures, liquids, or electromagnetic interference. Ensure no foreign objects or residues are obstructing the pump mechanisms.

steps.title: Review Error Codes and Documentation
steps.instructions: Record the exact system error or fault code displayed. Check the BD Alaris service manual or internal troubleshooting resources for code-specific guidance.

steps.title: Reset or Update Software (If Applicable)
steps.instructions: Use manufacturer-approved procedures to reset the pump’s software if the system allows. Do not attempt firmware updates without following BD’s official instructions.

CCR.Complaint: Pump displayed 'System Error 123' during infusion and stopped delivering medication.
CCR.Cause: Pump failed self-test after power cycle; battery and AC connections verified; external factors ruled out.
CCR.Resolution: Device removed from service, labeled Out of Service, and sent to BD for bench evaluation.

title: BD MicroLab Spirometry Pulmonary Function Tester - Flow Sensor Calibration Errors
description: Troubleshooting flow sensor calibration errors caused by contamination, setup issues, or environmental factors.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm the device is not actively in use on a patient. Move testing to another spirometer if needed.

steps.title: Verify Calibration Prompt and Error Message
steps.instructions: Review the exact calibration error message on the screen and attempt calibration again to confirm repeatability.

steps.title: Check Flow Sensor Installation
steps.instructions: Remove and reseat the flow sensor, ensuring proper orientation, full connection, and no looseness.

steps.title: Inspect Flow Sensor for Contamination or Moisture
steps.instructions: Check for condensation, debris, mucus, or damage. Replace with a clean or new sensor if needed.

steps.title: Verify Correct Sensor Type and Accessories
steps.instructions: Confirm correct flow sensor, mouthpiece, and filters are installed and compatible.

steps.title: Check Calibration Syringe (if used)
steps.instructions: Ensure correct volume syringe, no leaks, and smooth full strokes during calibration.

steps.title: Evaluate Environmental Conditions
steps.instructions: Check for extreme temperatures, humidity, and strong airflow from vents or fans.

steps.title: Restart the Device
steps.instructions: Power cycle the unit and retry calibration after full initialization.

steps.title: Try Alternate Flow Sensor (if available)
steps.instructions: Use a known good sensor to confirm whether the original sensor has failed.

CCR.Complaint: Unit failed flow sensor calibration during daily spirometry setup.
CCR.Cause: Contaminated flow sensor with moisture affecting airflow measurement.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced flow sensor and successfully completed calibration.

title: BD MicroLab Spirometry Pulmonary Function Tester - Patient Interface Not Detected
description: Troubleshooting patient interface detection issues on BD MicroLab Spirometry systems by identifying connection, accessory, and sensor-related causes.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm the device is not actively in use on a patient. Move testing to another spirometer if needed.

steps.title: Verify Error Message and System Status
steps.instructions: Confirm the exact “patient interface not detected” message. Attempt to reconnect and observe if the message clears.

steps.title: Check Patient Interface Connection
steps.instructions: Inspect the connection between the flow sensor/patient interface and the device. Ensure it is fully inserted, not loose, and properly oriented. Disconnect and firmly reconnect.

steps.title: Inspect Cable and Connector Condition
steps.instructions: Check for bent or damaged pins, frayed or kinked cable, and debris in connectors. Replace the cable or interface if damage is found.

steps.title: Verify Correct Patient Interface / Sensor Type
steps.instructions: Ensure the correct BD-compatible flow sensor or patient interface is being used.

steps.title: Inspect Flow Sensor for Contamination or Moisture
steps.instructions: Check for condensation, debris or residue, or blockage. Replace or clean per facility protocol if needed.

steps.title: Power Cycle the Device
steps.instructions: Turn the unit off, wait 10–15 seconds, then power back on. Reconnect the patient interface after reboot.

steps.title: Try a Known Good Patient Interface
steps.instructions: Swap with a confirmed working flow sensor/interface to determine if the issue is accessory-related or device-related.

CCR.Complaint: Unit reports “patient interface not detected” when attempting spirometry testing.
CCR.Cause: Failed flow sensor interface due to damaged connector pins.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced patient interface with known good unit; device returned to service and verified operational.

title: BD MicroLab Spirometry Pulmonary Function Tester - Software Freeze or PC Connection Lost
description: Troubleshooting software freezing and PC communication loss issues on BD MicroLab spirometry systems.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm the device is not actively being used on a patient and move testing to another spirometer if needed.

steps.title: Observe System Behavior
steps.instructions: Determine if the issue is a complete system freeze, intermittent freezing, or loss of communication with PC/software.

steps.title: Perform a Controlled Restart
steps.instructions: Close the spirometry software if responsive and reboot the PC or device properly.

steps.title: Verify Physical Connection to PC
steps.instructions: Check all communication cables for secure connection, damage, and reseat connections firmly.

steps.title: Check USB Port / Communication Port
steps.instructions: Try a different USB port, avoid hubs, and confirm the device is recognized by the PC.

steps.title: Confirm Software is Running Properly
steps.instructions: Restart the application, check for errors, and verify the correct device is selected.

steps.title: Check PC Performance and Background Processes
steps.instructions: Close unnecessary applications and ensure the PC is not overloaded or lagging.

steps.title: Inspect Power Stability
steps.instructions: Ensure stable power connections and check for loose or intermittent power sources.

steps.title: Test with Known Good Cable (if available)
steps.instructions: Replace the communication cable with a known working one to rule out cable failure.

CCR.Complaint: Spirometry system freezing during testing and losing connection to PC software.
CCR.Cause: Loose USB communication cable intermittently disconnecting device from PC.
CCR.Resolution: Reseated and secured USB cable; verified stable communication and normal operation.

helpfulDetails: USB/communication cable condition and connection status
Final device status (operational or removed from service)

title: BD MicroLab Spirometry Pulmonary Function Tester - Network / EMR communication failure
description: Troubleshooting EMR communication failures caused by network issues, configuration errors, or software/interface problems.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm no active test is in progress, ensure all results are saved locally, and move testing to another spirometer if needed.

steps.title: Verify Network Connection (Physical and Status)
steps.instructions: Check Ethernet cable is fully seated with active link lights or confirm Wi-Fi connection is active.

steps.title: Confirm PC or Device Network Access
steps.instructions: Attempt to access hospital network drives or internal websites to confirm connectivity.

steps.title: Verify EMR / Interface Software Status
steps.instructions: Ensure spirometry software and interface applications are open, responsive, and free of errors; restart if needed.

steps.title: Check Patient Data Transmission Workflow
steps.instructions: Attempt to send a test record and observe for failures, delays, or error messages.

steps.title: Verify Correct Configuration Settings
steps.instructions: Confirm EMR destination, IP/hostname, and port settings match known working configuration without making unverified changes.

steps.title: Check Date and Time Settings
steps.instructions: Verify system date and time are accurate to prevent authentication or data rejection issues.

steps.title: Restart System and Reinitialize Connection
steps.instructions: Restart the device or PC and associated software to reset communication sessions.

steps.title: Check for Network or EMR Downtime
steps.instructions: Confirm with IT or staff whether EMR systems, interface engines, or network services are down or under maintenance.

CCR.Complaint: Spirometry results not transmitting to EMR.
CCR.Cause: Network cable found loose at workstation causing intermittent connection loss.
CCR.Resolution: Reseated Ethernet cable and verified successful transmission to EMR.

helpfulDetails: Network cable condition and connection status

title: BD MicroLab Spirometry Pulmonary Function Tester - Inaccurate spirometry readings
description: Identifies external causes of inaccurate spirometry results such as calibration issues, leaks, contamination, incorrect setup, or environmental factors.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm the device is not actively in use on a patient. Move testing to another spirometer if needed.

steps.title: Verify Calibration Status
steps.instructions: Check when the last calibration was performed. Perform a new calibration using a known good calibration syringe.

steps.title: Confirm Proper Calibration Technique
steps.instructions: Ensure calibration strokes are smooth and consistent (no hesitation or variable speed). Verify correct syringe volume is selected in the system.

steps.title: Inspect Flow Sensor for Contamination or Moisture
steps.instructions: Check for condensation inside the sensor, debris, mucus, or residue, and visible wear or damage. Replace or clean per facility protocol if contamination is present.

steps.title: Check Patient Interface and Tubing
steps.instructions: Inspect mouthpiece, tubing, and connections for loose fittings, cracks or leaks, and improper assembly. Replace disposable components if needed.

steps.title: Verify Correct Patient Setup and Entry
steps.instructions: Confirm patient demographics entered correctly (age, height, gender) and proper test selection and settings.

steps.title: Observe Patient Technique (If Applicable)
steps.instructions: Confirm proper seal around mouthpiece, no air leakage from nose (use of nose clip if required), and correct effort and coaching.

steps.title: Check Environmental Conditions
steps.instructions: Verify room temperature and humidity within acceptable range and no recent rapid temperature changes.

steps.title: Restart System and Software
steps.instructions: Perform a controlled restart of the device or connected PC.

CCR.Complaint: Staff reported spirometry results appeared inconsistent and lower than expected values.
CCR.Cause: Flow sensor contamination and minor air leak at tubing connection identified during inspection.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced tubing, cleaned sensor per protocol, performed successful calibration verification, and returned unit to service.

title: BD Alaris Infusion Pump - Error: 222.4010 High infusion rate detected
description: Troubleshooting guide for Error 222.4010 indicating high infusion rate detection causing pump stoppage to prevent over-infusion.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Stop infusion immediately and transfer patient therapy to a backup pump.

steps.title: Verify Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Confirm Error 222.4010 and observe when and how it occurs.

steps.title: Check Infusion Programming
steps.instructions: Verify infusion rate, VTBI, and ensure no programming errors.

steps.title: Verify Drug Library Settings
steps.instructions: Ensure correct drug profile and limits are not exceeded.

steps.title: Inspect Administration Set
steps.instructions: Check correct set installation and proper tubing alignment.

steps.title: Check for Free Flow
steps.instructions: Ensure no gravity-assisted or uncontrolled flow is occurring.

steps.title: Check for Occlusions
steps.instructions: Inspect for pressure fluctuations or occlusion release events.

steps.title: Power Cycle Device
steps.instructions: Restart pump to clear temporary faults.

steps.title: Test with Known Good Setup
steps.instructions: Use new tubing and test infusion off patient.

CCR.Complaint: BD Alaris pump displaying Error: 222.4010 and stopping infusion.
CCR.Cause: Verified programming and tubing setup; no external issues found. Suspected internal flow sensing fault.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced administration set and confirmed settings; error persisted. Removed pump from service and sent for repair.

title: BD Alaris Infusion Pump - Error: 222.4020 Low infusion rate detected
description: Error 222.4020 indicates low infusion rate on the BD Alaris pump, potentially causing under-infusion and delayed therapy.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Stop infusion, transfer patient to backup pump, and confirm patient stability.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Confirm error is displayed and observe when it occurs and how the pump behaves.

steps.title: Check Administration Set Installation
steps.instructions: Ensure correct set is used, properly loaded, aligned, and door fully latched.

steps.title: Inspect for Flow Restrictions
steps.instructions: Check tubing for clamps, kinks, air, and downstream occlusions.

steps.title: Verify Fluid Source and Setup
steps.instructions: Confirm adequate fluid, proper bag height, and correct venting.

steps.title: Confirm Programmed Infusion Parameters
steps.instructions: Review infusion rate and mode for appropriate settings.

steps.title: Check Pump Module Operation
steps.instructions: Observe for smooth operation and absence of abnormal sounds.

steps.title: Power Cycle the Device
steps.instructions: Restart pump and retest after reloading administration set.

CCR.Complaint: BD Alaris pump displaying Error 222.4020 and infusing slower than expected.
CCR.Cause: Administration set misloaded or tubing restriction causing reduced flow rate.
CCR.Resolution: Reseated set and cleared restriction or removed device from service for repair.

title: BD Alaris Infusion Pump - Error: 222.4030 Motor stall detected
description: Error 222.4030 indicates a motor stall condition that stops infusion due to resistance, improper setup, or internal pump failure.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Stop infusion, remove pump from use, and transfer therapy to a functioning device.

steps.title: Verify Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Confirm error message and observe when it occurs and any abnormal sounds.

steps.title: Check Tubing Load
steps.instructions: Inspect and properly reload administration set ensuring correct routing and seating.

steps.title: Check for Occlusion
steps.instructions: Inspect tubing, clamps, and patient line for any resistance or blockage.

steps.title: Inspect Pump Channel
steps.instructions: Check for contamination, debris, or restricted movement inside the pump channel.

steps.title: Check Door Mechanism
steps.instructions: Ensure door closes fully and latches properly without misalignment.

steps.title: Test with Known Good Setup
steps.instructions: Use a new administration set and verify proper pump operation.

steps.title: Swap Pump Module
steps.instructions: Move setup to another module to isolate device vs setup issue.

CCR.Complaint: Alaris pump displaying Error 222.4030 Motor stall detected and would not start infusion.
CCR.Cause: Tubing and occlusion ruled out; error persisted with known good setup, indicating internal motor failure.
CCR.Resolution: Removed pump module from service and sent for repair.

title: BD Alaris Infusion Pump - Error: 222.4040 Encoder out of sync with motor pulses
description: Motor and encoder mismatch error causing infusion inaccuracy or pump stoppage.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove pump from patient use and transition infusion to a backup device.

steps.title: Verify Error
steps.instructions: Confirm Error 222.4040 and observe when and how it occurs.

steps.title: Check IV Set and Module Seating
steps.instructions: Reload tubing properly and ensure module is fully seated.

steps.title: Inspect Pump Channel
steps.instructions: Check for debris, residue, or obstruction and clean if needed.

steps.title: Test with Known Good IV Set
steps.instructions: Use a new approved set and run a test infusion off patient.

steps.title: Observe Motor Operation
steps.instructions: Verify smooth motor movement with no irregular sounds or motion.

steps.title: Power Cycle Device
steps.instructions: Restart pump and reseat module to clear transient faults.

CCR.Complaint: BD Alaris pump displaying Error 222.4040 and unable to maintain infusion.
CCR.Cause: External setup verified; error persisted indicating motor/encoder mismatch or internal fault.
CCR.Resolution: Removed from service and sent for repair evaluation.

title: BD Alaris Infusion Pump - Error: 222.4060 Over-infusion state detected (infusion past VTBI limit)
description: Error 222.4060 indicating infusion exceeded VTBI limit, causing pump stoppage to prevent over-infusion and potential patient harm.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Stop infusion, remove pump from use, assess patient, and transfer therapy to another device.

steps.title: Verify Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Confirm error display and determine when and how the event occurred.

steps.title: Check VTBI Programming
steps.instructions: Compare programmed VTBI, rate, and mode with physician order and infused volume.

steps.title: Inspect Set Loading
steps.instructions: Verify tubing is correctly seated, door is latched, and proper set is used.

steps.title: Check for Free-Flow
steps.instructions: Ensure clamps and anti-free-flow mechanisms are functioning and no unintended flow occurs.

steps.title: Inspect Flow Conditions
steps.instructions: Check for kinks, occlusions, air, and improper setup.

steps.title: Replace Administration Set
steps.instructions: Install new set and observe if issue resolves.

steps.title: Perform Functional Test
steps.instructions: Run controlled infusion to confirm accurate VTBI stop.

CCR.Complaint: Pump displayed Error 222.4060 with concern of infusion exceeding VTBI.
CCR.Cause: Incorrect programming, tubing issue, or internal control failure after verification.
CCR.Resolution: Verified setup, replaced set, tested function; removed from service if unresolved.

title: BD Alaris Infusion Pump - Error: 222.4200 Motor activity detected when motor should be idle

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Observe when error occurs, listen for motor noise, and check for movement while idle.

steps.title: Identify the Affected Module
steps.instructions: Determine if issue is with infusion module or PC unit and swap modules if needed.

steps.title: Check Administration Set Installation
steps.instructions: Reseat the set, confirm proper loading, and inspect for kinks or tension.

steps.title: Inspect Pump Channel and Mechanism
steps.instructions: Check for debris, contamination, or mechanical interference.

steps.title: Power Cycle the System
steps.instructions: Restart the device and observe for normal initialization.

steps.title: Check Environmental or Handling Factors
steps.instructions: Assess for drops, vibration, or improper mounting.

steps.title: Swap Administration Set
steps.instructions: Install a new known-good set to rule out resistance issues.

steps.title: Perform Functional Test (Off Patient)
steps.instructions: Run a test infusion and verify motor remains idle when stopped.

CCR.Complaint: BD Alaris infusion module displaying Error 222.4200 with motor activity while idle.
CCR.Cause: Unintended motor movement observed with no infusion running. Issue persisted after set replacement and module swap, indicating internal fault.

CCR.Complaint: BD Alaris pump displaying Error 242.4010 with high infusion rate alarm during use.
