title: BD BBS Revolution Bladder Scanner - Wireless scanner not connecting to console
description: Troubleshooting BBS Revolution wireless scanner connection issues caused by battery, docking, Bluetooth connection, range, or console power problems.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions:
Confirm the scanner is not being used for an active patient assessment.

If staff need an immediate bladder volume, provide another verified bladder scanner or follow the department’s backup process.

Expected outcome: Patient care is not delayed while Clinical Engineering evaluates the scanner.

steps.title: Confirm the Reported Connection Problem
steps.instructions:
Ask staff what they observed:

Scanner does not connect after being removed from dock
Console does not wake up
Scanner button does not initiate scan
Blue scanner LEDs do not become solid
Scanner connects intermittently
Console does not display scan result
Scanner was found away from the console or not charged

Expected outcome: The failure mode is clearly identified before troubleshooting.

steps.title: Verify Console Power and Battery Status
steps.instructions:
Check whether the console powers on and whether the console battery is low.

Connect the console to a known-good AC outlet and allow it to charge if the battery indicator is low. BD documentation notes the console should be connected to AC power daily, and a red battery indicator means the device should be connected to AC for at least two hours.

Expected outcome: Console power is stable and not preventing wireless communication.

If restoring AC power resolves the issue, stop and document the outlet, cord, or charging-related cause.

steps.title: Check Scanner Battery and Docking Position
steps.instructions:
Dock the wireless scanner into the console and verify that yellow lights appear on the scanner, indicating it is charging.

Confirm the scanner is seated in the correct orientation. If the scanner is docked backwards, the scanner will not charge, and the console may show a rotating or blinking scanner icon.

Expected outcome: Scanner charging indication is present.

If the scanner was docked incorrectly and begins charging after repositioning, allow adequate charge time, retest, and stop if the connection returns.

steps.title: Wake the Scanner Properly
steps.instructions:
Remove the scanner from the dock and press the scanner button.

Wait for the blue LEDs to become solid, which indicates a successful Bluetooth connection according to the BD operating information.

Expected outcome: Scanner wakes and establishes communication with the console.

If the blue LEDs become solid and scanning works, document as a scanner sleep or low-power recovery issue.

steps.title: Check Wireless Range and Location
steps.instructions:
Keep the scanner and console close together during testing.

The BBS Revolution uses a wireless scanner and display console, with scan results transferred wirelessly to the console. BD in-service material lists the wireless connection range as approximately 33 ft / 10 m.

Expected outcome: Scanner connects reliably when tested near the console.

If the scanner only fails at distance, document range, wall, room, or interference concerns rather than replacing parts immediately.

steps.title: Inspect the Scanner and Console Dock Area
steps.instructions:
Look for external damage, fluid residue, dried gel, cracked housing, damaged charging contacts, loose dock fit, or debris preventing full seating.

Clean external surfaces according to facility-approved cleaning procedures. Do not scrape, pry, or disassemble the dock or scanner.

Expected outcome: Scanner seats fully and charges without obstruction.

If cleaning and proper seating restore connection, stop and document the finding.

steps.title: Power Cycle the System
steps.instructions:
With the scanner docked, power the console down if possible, disconnect AC briefly, then reconnect AC and power the console back on.

Remove the scanner and press the scanner button to reattempt connection.

Expected outcome: Console and scanner reinitialize and reconnect.

If a temporary software or communication lockup clears after restart, document the power cycle and verify repeated successful connection.

steps.title: Verify Scanner-to-Console Pairing Behavior
steps.instructions:
Confirm the scanner belongs with that console and was not swapped from another BBS Revolution unit.

If multiple units are in the area, check asset tags and physical pairing history. A wireless scanner from another cart may not communicate as expected with the current console.

Expected outcome: The correct scanner is being tested with its intended console.

If the wrong scanner was docked or mixed between units, return components to the correct asset and retest.

steps.title: Check for Intermittent Connection
steps.instructions:
Perform several dock-undock and scan-start attempts near the console.

Watch for inconsistent LED behavior, delayed connection, scanner shutting off quickly, console failing to wake, or connection dropping after movement.

Expected outcome: Connection is repeatable and stable.

If the issue is intermittent but repeatable, remove the unit from service for bench evaluation.

steps.title: Inspect for Signs of Damage or Contamination
steps.instructions:
Check the scanner face, housing, button, LEDs, console dock, display, AC adapter, and charging cord.

Look for:

Cracks
Loose scanner button
Missing or inconsistent LEDs
Liquid intrusion signs
Damaged dock contacts
Bent or loose charging connection
Unusual heat or smell

Expected outcome: No visible damage or unsafe condition is present.

If damage, overheating, liquid intrusion, or unsafe charging behavior is found, stop troubleshooting and remove the device from service.

steps.title: Perform a Functional Connection Check
steps.instructions:
After charging, remove the scanner, confirm connection LEDs, perform a test scan according to facility policy, and verify that the console displays the result.

Expected outcome: Scanner connects, scan initiates, and result transfers to the console.

If connection and result transfer are normal after external checks, return the unit to service per department procedure.

CCR.Complaint: Nursing reported that the BD BBS Revolution wireless scanner would not connect to the console after being removed from the dock.
CCR.Cause: Scanner was found docked backwards and was not charging; yellow charging lights were not present until the scanner was reseated correctly.
CCR.Resolution: Repositioned scanner in the dock, confirmed charging indication, allowed unit to charge, verified blue connection LEDs, completed functional scan transfer, and returned device to service.

helpfulDetails: Scanner charging lights present or absent
Whether scanner was mixed with another console
Any visible damage, gel residue, or liquid contamination

title: BD BBS Revolution Bladder Scanner - Scanner not charging in dock
description: Troubleshooting BBS Revolution charging issues caused by dock power, scanner seating, contacts, cables, battery, or external damage.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm the bladder scanner is not being used for an active patient assessment. If the unit is needed for patient care, provide clinical staff with another verified bladder scanner before troubleshooting. Expected outcome: Patient care continues without relying on a scanner with uncertain battery status.

steps.title: Confirm the Reported Charging Problem
steps.instructions: Ask staff what they observed. Check whether the issue is: Scanner does not show charging when placed in the dock; Battery icon does not change; Scanner powers off quickly after docking; Dock has no power indicator; Charging is intermittent; Scanner only charges when held at an angle; Scanner does not power on after being docked. Expected outcome: The exact failure mode is confirmed before parts are blamed.

steps.title: Verify the Dock Has Power
steps.instructions: Check that the charging dock is plugged into a known working outlet. If the dock uses an external power supply, confirm the power supply is firmly connected at both the wall outlet and the dock. Expected outcome: Dock power is present and the charging issue is not due to an unplugged or failed outlet. If reconnecting power restores charging, stop and document the loose or disconnected power source.

steps.title: Test the Wall Outlet
steps.instructions: Plug another device or an outlet tester into the same receptacle. Avoid assuming the scanner or dock has failed until the outlet is confirmed. Expected outcome: The outlet provides proper power. If the outlet is dead or controlled by a switch, move the dock to a verified outlet and retest.

steps.title: Inspect the External Power Supply and Cord
steps.instructions: Look for: Bent connector pins; Damaged insulation; Strain at the cable ends; Loose barrel connector; Cracked power supply housing; Signs of overheating or smell. Expected outcome: The power supply and cable are physically intact. If damage, heat, or odor is found, remove the charger from service and do not continue using it.

steps.title: Confirm the Scanner Is Seated Correctly in the Dock
steps.instructions: Place the BBS Revolution scanner into the charging dock and verify it sits fully and evenly. Check for obstruction from labels, cleaning residue, debris, or misalignment. Expected outcome: The scanner makes proper contact with the charging dock. If reseating the scanner restores charging, stop and document improper seating or obstruction.

steps.title: Inspect Charging Contacts
steps.instructions: Inspect the scanner and dock charging contacts for: Dirt; Fluid residue; Corrosion; Bent contacts; Worn contact surfaces; Broken or recessed contact points. Clean external contact surfaces only according to facility-approved cleaning practices and manufacturer-compatible disinfectants. Expected outcome: Charging contacts are clean, dry, and physically intact. If bent, broken, corroded, or recessed contacts are found, remove the unit or dock from service for repair evaluation.

steps.title: Check for Cleaning or Fluid Intrusion Issues
steps.instructions: Ask whether the scanner or dock was recently cleaned, soaked, sprayed heavily, or stored wet. Moisture around charging contacts can prevent charging and may create an electrical safety concern. Expected outcome: No moisture or fluid contamination is present. If liquid intrusion is suspected, remove the device and dock from service and send for evaluation.

steps.title: Allow Time for Charging Response
steps.instructions: If the battery is deeply discharged, place the scanner in the powered dock and allow it to remain seated long enough to see whether the charging indicator appears. Expected outcome: The scanner begins showing charging status after being docked. If there is still no indication of charging after a reasonable observation period, continue troubleshooting.

steps.title: Check for Intermittent Charging
steps.instructions: Gently observe whether the charging indicator changes when the scanner is lightly repositioned in the dock. Do not force the scanner, bend contacts, or apply pressure to make it charge. Expected outcome: Charging remains stable without needing to hold the scanner in a specific position. If charging only occurs at certain angles, suspect worn dock contacts, scanner contact damage, or dock alignment failure.

steps.title: Compare With a Known Good Dock or Power Supply, If Available
steps.instructions: If another compatible BBS Revolution charging dock or approved power supply is available, test the scanner using the known good setup. Also test a known good scanner on the suspect dock if available. Expected outcome: The issue follows either the scanner, dock, or power supply. If the scanner charges on another dock, the original dock or power supply is likely the problem. If another scanner charges on the same dock, the original scanner or battery is likely the problem.

steps.title: Inspect the Scanner Battery Behavior
steps.instructions: After attempting to charge, power on the scanner and observe battery status. Look for: Battery level does not increase; Battery drains quickly; Battery icon is missing or abnormal; Scanner shuts off immediately when removed from dock; Battery or charging error message. Expected outcome: Battery status responds normally to charging. If the battery does not accept or hold charge after external causes are ruled out, the scanner should be evaluated for battery or internal charging circuit failure.

steps.title: Check for Physical Damage
steps.instructions: Inspect the scanner and dock for drops, cracks, loose housings, damaged alignment features, or broken plastic around the charging area. Expected outcome: No visible damage affects docking or charging. If damage is present, remove the affected component from service and send for repair evaluation.

steps.title: Perform a Functional Check After Charging Is Restored
steps.instructions: If charging resumes, allow the unit to charge and then verify basic operation. Confirm: Scanner powers on; Battery level displays normally; Charging indicator behaves correctly; Unit remains powered after removal from dock; No charging alarms or error messages return. Expected outcome: The scanner charges and functions normally before being returned to service.

CCR.Complaint: Clinical staff reported the BD BBS Revolution bladder scanner would not charge when placed in the docking station.
CCR.Cause: Verified outlet power was present, but the scanner only charged intermittently when repositioned in the dock, indicating a likely dock contact or alignment issue.
CCR.Resolution: Removed the dock and scanner from service, labeled them Out of Service, and sent the system for bench evaluation.

helpfulDetails: Known-good dock or power supply tested
Battery icon or charging indicator behavior
Physical damage to scanner or dock

title: BD BBS Revolution Bladder Scanner - Automatic bladder detection failure
description: Troubleshooting failed bladder auto-detection caused by positioning, battery, probe contact, patient factors, setup, or device condition.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm the scanner is not being used in a situation where an inaccurate bladder volume could delay necessary clinical care. If the patient’s condition requires urgent assessment, advise clinical staff to use an alternate verified bladder scanner or follow facility policy for further evaluation. Expected outcome: Patient care is not delayed by troubleshooting.

steps.title: Confirm the Reported Detection Problem
steps.instructions: Ask clinical staff what they observed during scanning. Common reports may include: Bladder not automatically detected; Scanner unable to center or locate bladder; Repeated low or inconsistent volume readings; Scan completes but displays poor confidence or questionable results; Device requires repeated rescans to produce a usable result. Expected outcome: The exact failure mode is understood before troubleshooting begins.

steps.title: Check Battery Status and Power Condition
steps.instructions: Verify the BBS Revolution has adequate battery charge and powers on normally. Low battery or unstable power can affect scan completion, display response, and system performance. Expected outcome: Device powers on normally and has enough charge to complete multiple scans. If charging or power is the issue, place the unit on the correct charger or remove it from service if it will not charge properly.

steps.title: Inspect the Probe and Scanner Housing
steps.instructions: Examine the probe, cable, connector area, display, and scanner body for visible damage. Look for: Cracked probe face; Damaged cable jacket; Loose or strained connector; Fluid intrusion; Impact damage; Damaged screen or controls. Expected outcome: No physical damage is found that would affect scanning. If damage is found, stop troubleshooting and remove the device from service.

steps.title: Verify Probe Connection and Seating
steps.instructions: Confirm the probe is fully connected and properly seated, if applicable to the configuration. A loose or intermittent probe connection can cause poor image acquisition or failed automatic bladder detection. Expected outcome: Probe connection is secure and the scanner recognizes the probe normally. If reseating the probe restores normal detection, document the loose connection and stop.

steps.title: Confirm Proper Scan Setup
steps.instructions: Review the scanner setup with clinical staff. Verify the correct patient type or scan mode is selected, if available, and confirm that the device is being used according to facility workflow. Expected outcome: The scanner is not failing because of an incorrect mode, setup selection, or workflow error.

steps.title: Check Coupling Gel Use
steps.instructions: Confirm adequate ultrasound gel is being applied between the probe and the patient. Insufficient gel or poor probe contact can prevent the scanner from obtaining a clear image for automatic bladder recognition. Expected outcome: Gel coverage is adequate and probe contact is consistent. If proper gel use restores automatic detection, stop and document the correction.

steps.title: Check Probe Positioning Technique
steps.instructions: Have qualified clinical staff confirm probe placement and scan angle. Automatic bladder detection may fail if the probe is too high, too low, angled away from the bladder, or not centered over the expected bladder location. Expected outcome: The scanner can identify the bladder when the probe is positioned correctly. If repositioning resolves the issue, document user technique or positioning as the cause.

steps.title: Consider Patient-Specific Factors
steps.instructions: Confirm whether the issue occurs on one patient only or across multiple patients. Patient factors that may affect detection include: Very low bladder volume; Obesity or increased tissue depth; Abdominal dressings; Scarring; Pregnancy; Ascites; Unusual anatomy; Difficulty positioning the probe. Expected outcome: The issue is separated from patient-specific scanning limitations. If the problem occurs only under difficult patient conditions, the device may be functioning normally.

steps.title: Perform a Controlled Test Scan
steps.instructions: Test the scanner using an approved training phantom, test fixture, or another facility-approved verification method if available. Do not use an active patient for repeated equipment troubleshooting unless clinical staff confirm it is appropriate. Expected outcome: The scanner detects the target consistently under controlled conditions. If the scanner works during controlled testing, the problem is likely related to technique, setup, or patient-specific conditions.

steps.title: Compare With Another Known-Good Scanner
steps.instructions: If available, compare results using another verified bladder scanner under the same general conditions. This helps determine whether the issue follows the device or is related to patient condition or technique. Expected outcome: The suspect unit either performs similarly to the known-good unit or shows a repeatable failure. If only the BBS Revolution fails to detect automatically, remove it from service.

steps.title: Restart the Device
steps.instructions: Power the scanner off, wait briefly, and power it back on. A restart may clear a temporary software or sensor recognition issue. Expected outcome: The scanner boots normally and automatic detection functions correctly. If restart resolves the issue, document the reset and monitor for recurrence.

steps.title: Check for Repeated or Intermittent Failure
steps.instructions: Perform several test scans under safe, controlled conditions. Watch for intermittent detection, freezing, delayed response, probe recognition errors, or inconsistent scan confidence. Expected outcome: Automatic bladder detection is repeatable and stable. If detection remains inconsistent, remove the scanner from service for bench evaluation.

CCR.Complaint: Clinical staff reported that the BD BBS Revolution bladder scanner would not automatically detect the bladder during patient scans.
CCR.Cause: Troubleshooting found the probe and power were normal, but the device repeatedly failed automatic detection during controlled test scans.
CCR.Resolution: Removed the bladder scanner from service, labeled it Out of Service, and sent it for bench evaluation/repair.

helpfulDetails: Alarm behavior or detection prompts observed
Accessories swapped or compared, including charger, dock, or known-good scanner if available
Power behavior, including battery charge level verified and charger or dock checked
Environmental factors or patient-specific factors noted
Indicator lights, display messages, scan confidence, or error messages observed

title: BD BBS Revolution Bladder Scanner - Console touchscreen or display failure
description: Troubleshooting BBS Revolution touchscreen, display, response, visibility, startup, or console control issues from external or user-accessible causes.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm the bladder scanner is not being used during active patient assessment. If the screen is frozen, blank, inaccurate, or not responding, do not rely on the unit for a bladder volume reading. Expected outcome: Patient care is not delayed or based on an unreliable display. If bladder volume assessment is clinically needed, have staff use another verified bladder scanner before troubleshooting continues.

steps.title: Confirm the Reported Display or Touchscreen Issue
steps.instructions: Ask clinical staff what occurred and when it happened. Check whether the issue is: Blank screen; Dim or unreadable display; Frozen screen; Touchscreen not responding; Delayed touch response; Incorrect menu selection; Display flickering; Console powers on but screen does not show expected information; Unit will not complete startup. Expected outcome: The failure mode is clearly identified before assuming a console failure.

steps.title: Check Power Status
steps.instructions: Verify that the console has adequate battery charge or is connected to its approved charging source. If the display is blank, place the unit on its charger or connect approved power and allow it to charge briefly before retesting. Expected outcome: The console powers on normally and the display becomes readable. If power restoration resolves the issue, stop and document the power or charging condition.

steps.title: Inspect the Charger and Power Connection
steps.instructions: Confirm the charger is fully seated, the power cord is connected, and the outlet is live. Try a known-good outlet if the charger does not appear to be providing power. Expected outcome: The charging indicator or console power behavior confirms that external power is available. If the issue follows the outlet or charger connection, correct that external issue and stop.

steps.title: Check for Physical Display Damage
steps.instructions: Inspect the screen for cracks, fluid intrusion, impact marks, delamination, or obvious pressure damage. Do not press hard on the touchscreen or attempt to force response. Expected outcome: Any visible damage is identified before further troubleshooting. If the display is cracked, fluid-contaminated, or physically damaged, remove the unit from service and send it for repair evaluation.

steps.title: Clean the Touchscreen Surface
steps.instructions: Check for gel, disinfectant residue, glove powder, adhesive, or moisture on the touchscreen. Clean the screen using facility-approved cleaning methods and allow it to dry fully before retesting. Expected outcome: Touch response returns if residue or moisture was interfering with touchscreen input. If cleaning resolves the issue, stop and document the finding.

steps.title: Remove External Interference From Accessories
steps.instructions: Confirm the probe, cable, cradle, or cart-mounted accessories are not pulling on the console or pressing against the screen. Reposition the console so nothing is contacting the touchscreen. Expected outcome: False touches, menu jumping, or unresponsive behavior clears if the screen was being physically interfered with.

steps.title: Perform a Controlled Restart
steps.instructions: Power the console off using the normal shutdown process if possible. If the screen is frozen but the device can be safely restarted, power cycle the unit according to normal user-accessible operation. Expected outcome: The console restarts, the display loads normally, and touchscreen function returns. If restart resolves the issue, stop and document that the console recovered after power cycle.

steps.title: Verify Startup Behavior
steps.instructions: Observe whether the console shows logo/startup screens, battery indicators, error messages, or normal menu options. Note whether the issue occurs immediately at power-on or only after scanning or menu navigation. Expected outcome: The problem is narrowed to startup, display visibility, touchscreen input, or software response behavior.

steps.title: Check Brightness or Display Visibility Settings
steps.instructions: If the display is on but difficult to see, confirm that brightness or screen timeout settings have not been changed. Also check for glare, room lighting, or privacy screen interference if applicable. Expected outcome: Display visibility improves after correcting settings or viewing conditions. If the screen remains dim, flickering, or unreadable, continue troubleshooting.

steps.title: Test Touchscreen Function Across Several Areas
steps.instructions: From a safe non-patient screen, check whether touch response fails everywhere or only in one region of the display. Avoid entering incorrect patient data or changing clinical settings during testing. Expected outcome: The touchscreen either responds normally, fails globally, or shows a localized dead area. A localized dead area usually supports removing the unit from service for repair.

steps.title: Check for Repeatability
steps.instructions: Restart the unit once more and verify whether the issue returns. Confirm whether the display failure occurs on battery, on charger, or both. Expected outcome: The failure pattern is documented clearly for repair or vendor support.

steps.title: Review for Recent Drops, Cleaning Events, or Fluid Exposure
steps.instructions: Ask staff whether the unit was dropped, cleaned heavily, exposed to liquid, or recently transported. This helps identify external causes that may explain display or touchscreen failure. Expected outcome: Recent handling or exposure history is captured for the work order.

steps.title: Remove From Service if Reliability Is Questionable
steps.instructions: If the screen remains unreadable, intermittently freezes, selects incorrect items, or fails to respond reliably, do not return the bladder scanner to clinical use. Expected outcome: An unreliable console is removed before it can affect patient care or documentation.

CCR.Complaint: Nursing reported the BD BBS Revolution bladder scanner touchscreen was not responding and the console could not be used to start a scan.
CCR.Cause: Found gel residue and moisture on the touchscreen surface; display powered on normally and responded after cleaning and drying.
CCR.Resolution: Cleaned touchscreen using approved method, verified normal menu response and startup operation, and returned the bladder scanner to service.

helpfulDetails: Display blank, dim, frozen, or flickering
Touchscreen completely unresponsive or partially responsive
Any visible cracks or fluid exposure
Whether gel or residue was present
Whether issue occurred on battery, charger, or both
Any error messages or startup behavior

title: BD BBS Revolution Bladder Scanner - Battery / pole-mount power issue
description: Troubleshooting battery charging, short runtime, dock power, pole-mount power, or startup issues from external causes.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm the bladder scanner is not actively being used for a patient scan. If the unit loses power during clinical use, have staff complete the scan with another verified bladder scanner before troubleshooting. Expected outcome: Patient care is not delayed, and troubleshooting is performed only when safe.

steps.title: Confirm the Reported Power Problem
steps.instructions: Ask clinical staff what was observed. Common reports may include: Scanner will not power on; Battery does not charge; Battery drains quickly; Unit powers off when removed from charger; Charging indicator does not appear; Pole-mount power connection seems loose; Scanner works on one dock or mount but not another. Expected outcome: The failure mode is clearly identified before testing.

steps.title: Check the Battery Charge Status
steps.instructions: Place the scanner on its charger, dock, or powered pole-mount connection and observe the battery or charging indicator. Confirm whether the scanner shows charging, low battery, no battery, or no power. Expected outcome: The device should indicate that external power is present and the battery is charging. If the scanner begins charging normally, allow it to charge fully, then retest runtime. If this resolves the issue, stop and document.

steps.title: Verify the Wall Outlet
steps.instructions: Plug another known-working device or outlet tester into the same outlet used by the charger or pole mount. Check for a loose receptacle, switched outlet, tripped power strip, or disconnected power source. Expected outcome: The outlet provides stable power. If the outlet is not powered, move the charger to a verified outlet. If the scanner charges normally, stop and document the outlet issue.

steps.title: Inspect the Power Supply and AC Cord
steps.instructions: Check the external power supply, AC cord, and charger connection for damage, looseness, bent pins, cuts, strain, or signs of overheating. Confirm the power brick is fully seated and that any detachable cord is firmly connected. Expected outcome: The power supply and cord are intact and properly connected. If the cord or power supply is damaged, remove it from service and replace with an approved compatible part.

steps.title: Check the Charger or Dock Seating
steps.instructions: Place the BBS Revolution scanner onto the charging base or mount and make sure it seats fully. Look for obstruction, debris, misalignment, or a loose fit that prevents charging contacts from mating properly. Expected outcome: The scanner sits securely and charging begins. If reseating the scanner restores charging, clean the exterior contact area as allowed by facility procedure, retest, and document.

steps.title: Inspect the Pole-Mount Power Setup
steps.instructions: If the scanner is mounted on a pole, verify that the pole-mount power cable, charger bracket, and docking interface are secure. Gently check for excessive movement, loose hardware, or a power cable that pulls away when the pole is moved. Expected outcome: The pole-mounted power path remains stable during normal handling. If movement causes charging to drop in and out, remove the mount from use and repair or replace the affected external component.

steps.title: Test with a Known-Good Charging Location
steps.instructions: Move the scanner to another known-good BD-compatible charger, dock, or power setup if available. Observe whether the same scanner begins charging normally. Expected outcome: A known-good charging setup helps separate a scanner problem from a charger, dock, mount, or power supply problem. If the scanner charges normally on another setup, the original charger, power supply, or pole mount is the likely issue.

steps.title: Test a Known-Good Scanner on the Suspect Charger
steps.instructions: If available, place another working BBS Revolution scanner on the suspect charger or pole-mount power setup. Expected outcome: A working scanner should charge normally on a good charger. If the known-good scanner also fails to charge, the issue is likely with the charger, dock, pole mount, power supply, or outlet rather than the scanner.

steps.title: Check for Battery Runtime Failure
steps.instructions: After a full charge, remove the scanner from external power and verify whether it powers on and remains on long enough for normal use. Expected outcome: The scanner should operate on battery without shutting down immediately. If the unit shuts off quickly after a verified full charge, the battery may be weak, failed, or not being properly managed by the device.

steps.title: Look for Signs of Battery or Power Safety Concerns
steps.instructions: Inspect the scanner and charging components for unusual heat, swelling, odor, discoloration, liquid intrusion, or damaged plastic. Expected outcome: No abnormal heat, odor, swelling, or damage is present. If any safety concern is found, stop troubleshooting, remove the device from service, label it Out of Service, and escalate for repair evaluation.

steps.title: Perform a Basic Functional Check After Power Is Restored
steps.instructions: Once charging or battery operation appears normal, power the scanner on and confirm normal startup, display operation, battery indication, and readiness for scanning. Expected outcome: The scanner powers on normally and remains stable on both external power and battery. If the power issue is resolved and no further fault is present, return the device to service per facility policy.

CCR.Complaint: Clinical staff reported that the BD BBS Revolution bladder scanner would not charge while mounted on the pole and powered off shortly after removal from the charger.
CCR.Cause: Verified outlet power was present, but charging was intermittent when the scanner was seated on the pole-mount dock due to a loose external power connection at the mount.
CCR.Resolution: Secured the pole-mount power connection, confirmed stable charging indicator, verified battery operation after charge, and returned the bladder scanner to service.

helpfulDetails: Whether another scanner charged on the same dock
Whether the scanner charged on another known-good dock
Any unusual heat, odor, swelling, or damage
